text.title: Go Error Handling Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying go-error-handling-patterns. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: API returns 500 for a not-found resource because errors.Is(err, ErrNotFound) returned false after a re-wrap with errors.New.
antipattern.root-cause: Author concatenated string instead of `%w`; chain lost; sentinel match failed.
antipattern.fix: Replace `errors.New("prefix: " + err.Error())` with `fmt.Errorf("prefix: %w", err)`. Add `errorlint` to golangci.

antipattern.symptom: Middleware fails to recognise a wrapped domain error and returns 500 instead of 409.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `var e *ConflictError; if errors.As(err, &e) { ... }`. Lint catches future occurrences.

antipattern.symptom: Handler panics on invalid input; recover middleware logs as 500.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated panic as a quick failure exit.
antipattern.fix: Replace panic with return apperror.ErrValidation. Handler returns; middleware translates to 400.

antipattern.symptom: One handler returns 404 for not-found; another returns 410. Customers report inconsistent API.
antipattern.root-cause: Each handler translated errors independently; conventions drifted.
antipattern.fix: Centralise translation in a single middleware/interceptor. Remove per-handler status mapping.
